zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisha E Smith於1754年5月18日出生在美國紐約州普特南卡梅爾時，他的父親David Smith28歲，母親Sarah Ruggles30歲。他於1775年6月19日與Rachel Hughson在美國紐約州維斯斯特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1849年6月26日在美國紐約州格林享年95歲，埋葬在美國紐約州格林凱洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisha E Smith于1754年5月18日出生在美国纽约州普特南卡梅爾时，他的父亲David Smith28岁，母亲Sarah Ruggles30岁。他于1775年6月19日与Rachel Hughson在美国纽约州維斯斯特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1849年6月26日在美国纽约州格林享年95岁，埋葬在美国纽约州格林凱洛。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Elisha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditionally a male Biblical name derived from Hebrew el ‘God’ + sha ‘to help or save’. It is borne in the Bible by a prophet, the disciple and successor of Elijah, whose story is told in the Second Book of Kings. It has long been popular among Christian fundamentalists as a boy's name, but is now increasingly frequent as a girl's name, perhaps influenced by Alicia .

variants.name: Eleesha
meanings: Traditionally a male Biblical name derived from Hebrew el ‘God’ + sha ‘to help or save’. It is borne in the Bible by a prophet, the disciple and successor of Elijah, whose story is told in the Second Book of Kings. It has long been popular among Christian fundamentalists as a boy's name, but is now increasingly frequent as a girl's name, perhaps influenced by Alicia .

nameMeanings.name: E
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鄂, a short form of present-day Hubei province: (i) from E (鄂), the name of an ancient state (located in Henan province), destroyed by the king Zhou, last king of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) from the placename E (鄂), the name of a fief (located in Shanxi province) in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the placename E (鄂), the name of a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to the royal members of the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) possibly a surname from minority ethnic groups in southwestern China. (v) said to be adopted as a new surname by Yue Ting, who changed his original name Yue (嶽) to E (鄂), because he fled to Huangmei in present-day Hubei province (called E (鄂) at that time) to avoid persecution after his father Yue Fei (1103–1142  AD ), a famous Chinese general in the Southern Song dynasty (1127 – 1279  AD ), was put to death by the then emperor.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.
Historical Boundaries: 1800: Greene, New York, United States
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

en.lifeSummary: When Elisha E Smith was born on 18 May 1754, in Carmel, Putnam, New York, United States, his father, David Smith, was 28 and his mother, Sarah Ruggles, was 30. He married Rachel Hughson on 19 June 1775, in Westchester, New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 26 June 1849, in Greene, New York, United States, at the age of 95, and was buried in Cairo, Greene, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisha E Smith est né le 18 mai 1754, à Carmel, Putnam, New York, États-Unis, son père, David Smith, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Sarah Ruggles, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Rachel Hughson le 19 juin 1775, à Westchester, New York, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 26 juin 1849, à Greene, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 95 ans, et a été enterré à Cairo, Greene, New York, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisha E Smith am 18. Mai 1754 in Carmel, Putnam, New York, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater David Smith 28 und seine Mutter Sarah Ruggles 30. Er hat am 19. Juni 1775 in Westchester, New York, Vereinigte Staaten Rachel Hughson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 26. Juni 1849 im Alter von 95 Jahren in Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Cairo, Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisha E Smith è nato il il 18 maggio 1754 nel Carmel, Putnam, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, David Smith, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Sarah Ruggles, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Rachel Hughson il 19 giugno 1775, nel Westchester, New York, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 26 giugno 1849, nel Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 95 anni, e fu sepolto a Cairo, Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisha E Smithが1754年5月18日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州プウトナムカーメルで生まれた時，父David Smithは28歳，母Sarah Rugglesは30歳であった。1775年6月19日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州ウエストチェスターでRachel Hughsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1849年6月26日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンで95歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンケイロにて埋葬された。

ko.Carmel, Putnam Co., NY: 미국 뉴욕 주 풋남 카멀 카멀
ko.Coxsackie, Greene, New York, United Stat: 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 콕스사키 콕스사키

ko.New Milford, Litchfield, Connecticut, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 뉴 미퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Elisha E Smith(이)가 1754년 5월 18일 미국 뉴욕 주 풋남 카멀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Smith(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Ruggles(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1775년 6월 19일 미국 뉴욕 주 웨스트체스터에서 Rachel Hughson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1849년 6월 26일 미국 뉴욕 주 그린에서 95세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 케어오에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisha E Smith nasceu em 18 de maio de 1754, em Carmel, Putnam, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, David Smith, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Ruggles, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Rachel Hughson em 19 de junho de 1775, em Westchester, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de junho de 1849, em Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 95 anos, e foi sepultado em Cairo, Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisha E Smith родился 18 мая 1754 г. в местности кармел, путнам, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени David Smith было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Ruggles, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Rachel Hughson 19 июня 1775 г. в местности весчестир, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 26 июня 1849 г. в местности грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 95 лет и был похоронен в местности цеиро, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisha E Smith nació el 18 de mayo de 1754, en Carmel, Putnam, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, su papá, David Smith, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Sarah Ruggles, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Rachel Hughson el 19 de junio de 1775, en Westchester, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 26 de junio de 1849, en Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 95 años, y fue sepultado en Cairo, Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
